chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Requires Full Blood Money and Half Blood Money in Cases Other Than the Taking of a Life

text: 5286 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَمِيلَةَ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ تَغْلِبَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "فِي اَلشَّفَةِ اَلسُّفْلَى سِتَّةُ آلاَفٍ وَ فِي اَلْعُلْيَا أَرْبَعَةُ آلاَفٍ لِأَنَّ اَلسُّفْلَى تُمْسِكُ اَلْمَاءَ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5286 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Jameelah from Aban ibn Taghlib from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"For the lower lip, the compensation is six thousand (dirhams), and for the upper lip, it is four thousand (dirhams) because the lower lip holds water."
